title: Best Restaurants in Trang Thailand (2026)
meta_description: The Trang restaurants that still matter: roast pork breakfasts, khanom jeen, tea-house culture, Kantang seafood, and old-town market eating backed by TAT sources.
intro: Trang food works best when you stop chasing scrape rankings and start planning the city the way Trang itself still does: breakfast first, one noodle or curry stop later, one slower tea or dessert break, and one real meal on the Kantang or market side if it fits the route.

items.name: Phong-O-Cha
items.description: The clearest breakfast anchor in Trang because TAT still frames it around roast pork, dim sum, rice porridge, and the city's old morning rhythm.
insider_tips: Go early and fold it into the station-side or old-town city route.
items.current_info: TAT still treats Phong-O-Cha as one of Trang's signature breakfast institutions.

items.name: Kha Nom Jeen Khun Pum
items.description: A stronger local noodle and curry stop than generic cafe filler, especially for travelers who want Trang's southern breakfast-to-lunch logic.
insider_tips: Best as a morning or lunch stop before heading out toward Kantang or the coast.
items.current_info: TAT's city article still uses Kha Nom Jeen Khun Pum as a practical Trang noodle and curry reference.

items.name: Cha Ko Jaeng Tea House
items.description: The best slower drinking stop in town when you want to see Trang's tea-house side rather than another interchangeable coffee bar.
highlights: Different from the usual cafe layer
insider_tips: Opening depends on the owner, so treat this as a bonus stop rather than a fixed appointment.
items.current_info: TAT highlights Cha Ko Jaeng specifically for its tea culture and owner-led brewing approach.

items.name: Ran Lor Kung
items.description: The strongest Kantang dining anchor because TAT still uses it to represent the district's Chinese-style seafood side.
insider_tips: Use this after the station and museum rather than as a standalone long drive.
items.current_info: TAT's Trang restaurant coverage still singles out Ran Lor Kung for Chinese seafood and its signature noodle dish with large prawns.

items.name: Talad Chin Ta
items.description: The best market-food layer in town when you want evening snacks, desserts, and a more lived-in city rhythm than a fixed top-table ranking.
insider_tips: Best treated as a grazing route instead of one single destination meal.
items.current_info: TAT still uses Talad Chin Ta as both a heritage street and a practical food-and-shopping layer.

items.name: Tabtieng Old Town Cafe & Bistro
items.description: A practical coffee-and-snack stop when you want a calmer old-town break without leaving the city core.
insider_tips: Use it as a pause between Talad Chin Ta and the market layer, not as the headline meal.
items.current_info: TAT's city article keeps Tabtieng Old Town Cafe & Bistro in the edited old-town food-and-coffee route.

items.name: Kuanito Patisserie
items.description: A polished pastry and coffee stop that adds one higher-finish city break without pushing Trang into generic brunch copy.
highlights: Works as a secondary city treat
insider_tips: Better as a dessert or late-afternoon coffee stop than as a meal anchor.
items.current_info: TAT's old-town route still uses Kuanito Patisserie as part of the edited city coffee-and-dessert layer.

content_sources.title: BEYOND THE BEACH : WHERE TO EAT, TRAVEL, AND SHOP AT IN TRANG
content_sources.creator: Tourism Authority of Thailand
